title: underground roasted Moroccan potatoes Gratin
description: A rich, creamy, and aromatic Moroccan-inspired dish featuring roasted charlotte potatoes with a fluffy top and a hint of spices.
instructions: Peel and slice the charlotte potatoes into 1/8-inch thick rounds.
In a large bowl, mix together the sliced potatoes, olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin powder, coriander powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Transfer the potato mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish and pour in the chicken broth.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden brown.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt and a pinch of salt.
Pour the yogurt mixture over the top of the potatoes and return the dish to the oven.
Bake for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until the top is creamy and set.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
